statement: Witness index contains 18 indexed witnesses/declarants; all are inquest testimony.
notes: CSV row count and statement_type field.

statement: WIT-0001 is unnamed in OCR, saw man near steps evening 30 Nov, communicated with Moss after seeing body next morning, maps to CHR-0006/CHR-0009 and EV-0014/EV-0005.
WIT-0001 cannot be used as settled facial identification.
Strapps and Neill reported evening observation around 7:20/7:30 p.m. with no movement and concern about the person.
Moss described first police attendance, body position, cigarette, no visible violence, transport, and initial property search.
Leane described identification inquiries, property, suitcase recovery, contents, tags, weather, circulation, and exhibit custody.
Durham photographed body, fingerprinted deceased, circulated materials, and photographed paper found on deceased.
Brown compared clothing, noted sand, received Tamam Shud slip from Leane, and made Rubaiyat inquiries.
Dwyer described postmortem examination at 7:30 a.m. on 2 Dec 1948 and medical observations/opinion.
Cowan received stomach contents, liver/muscle, urine and blood on 2 Dec 1948 and tested for common poisons without detecting any.
notes: Scope limited to common poisons/submitted specimens.

statement: Cleland gave medical and physical-evidence comparison testimony; observation, comparison, and opinion must be separated.
Hicks reviewed medical facts and discussed possible poison groups and timing difficulty; testimony must not identify a poison.
Bennett examined body about 9:40 a.m. on 1 Dec and estimated death could have occurred up to eight hours earlier.
Hall identified bus-ticket issue context; no holder or route-use identity established.
Holdernesse had no independent recollection apart from records/log and Hall statement.
North produced luggage-ticket/suitcase evidence but did not issue ticket; actual issuer absent.
West gave railway timetable context; this does not prove deceased travelled on any train.
Townsend gave Henley Beach ticket issue window and could not remember purchaser or exact issue time.
Lawson described making mould/cast on 7 Jun 1949; assumptions about body/feet are separated from direct creation.
Authorized witness index contains original inquest witnesses only and no modern researcher/genealogist witness entries.
notes: Later contributors excluded from this chapter.

statement: Reliability class counts are 6 strong, 10 moderate, 2 weak/limited, 0 contradicted as false, and 0 unknown.
Mission 6 indexed 55 transcription queue entries and noted OCR problems affecting names, exhibit numbers, medical terms, chemical terms, and property details.
Medical/scientific testimony retains scope limits: Cowan common-poison testing, Hicks possible poison groups, Bennett estimate, Cleland observation/opinion split.
